meta.reportLabel: Mon evening refresh — 60-day Iran roadmap + US oil waiver, oil 3-mo low / into PCE
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-strong / yen-weak. Warsh's hawkish FOMC stuck — DXY near 1-yr high ~100.8, dots flip next move to a HIKE. USD/JPY ~161.5 (near 1986 low), FinMin Katayama verbal-only.
meta.nextBigEvent: US PCE — Thu Jun 25 (the marquee test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call)

dailyRead: Two threads moved Monday and the picture held steady into the evening without breaking the dollar-strong / yen-weak regime. (1) Canada's May CPI ran hot AND broadened — headline 3.2% y/y (from 2.8%), but the tell is CPI-ex-gas rising to 2.2% (from 2.0%): pressure is no longer just energy, it's into food and stickier components. The loonie now has a firmer fundamental floor, so short-CAD legs stay trimmed. (2) The geopolitical binary got institutionalised — the US and Iran agreed a 60-day roadmap to a final deal (oversight/sanctions/nuclear working groups), agreed Hormuz stays toll-free for at least 60 days, and the US Treasury authorised Iranian oil production/sale for 60 days. The IRGC's Saturday re-closure claim was denied by the US military; markets ignored it, with Iran shipping the most oil through Hormuz since the conflict began. WTI sank to ~$74.3 (lowest since early March; Brent Aug ~$80.3), risk stayed bid, gold held ~$4,195 on a thin haven premium. Everything structural holds: DXY near 1-yr high ~100.8, USD/JPY ticked to ~161.5 with FinMin Katayama verbal-only and no confirmed intervention (intervention remains the JPY book's master variable), PCE Thursday still the marquee. Net: the oil-respike tail keeps shrinking, oil sits at a 3-month low, dollar-strong / yen-weak core unchanged — keep short-CAD trimmed, lean into risk-on / oil-lower, respect the crowded dollar into PCE.

strength.drivers: Core PCE firm; CPI 4.2% (May); claims 229k. Fed 3.50–3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE.
strength.forward: 🦅 Regime intact: median '26 dot ~3.8%, 9/18 see a hike. DXY 1-yr high. Next test: PCE Thu — a soft print is the only near-term fade. Crowded long.

strength.drivers: Core sticky; CPI 2.8% (soft headline, hot underneath). BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7–2 hold, 2 votes to HIKE, none to cut.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish dissent (Pill, Greene) + 'CPI may rise later' firmed GBP against a bid dollar. PMIs Tue the local catalyst. Next BoE Aug.

strength.drivers: Trimmed mean ~3.9%; unemp 4.5%. RBA 4.35%, hawkish hold, minutes: 'inflation risks to the upside'.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 RBA floor + risk-on beta vs a bid dollar. AUD CPI Wed + jobs Thu are the local catalysts. Next RBA Aug.

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; PPI hot. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16); spot squeezed ~161.5 (near 1986 low).
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination unchanged, but USD/JPY ~161.5 on the yield gap. FinMin Katayama verbal-only → actual intervention is the swing. Japan CPI Thu. Next BoJ late Jul.

strength.drivers: Headline easing; recovery establishing. RBNZ 2.25%; Q1 GDP firm +0.8%.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Firm GDP floors NZD; hawkish USD caps NZD/USD. Net neutral into Jul 8 RBNZ.

strength.drivers: May CPI hot + broadening: headline 3.2% (from 2.8%), CPI-ex-gas 2.2% (from 2.0%); gas +33.2%, groceries +4.3%. BoC 2.25% (parked).
strength.forward: ⚖️ Hot/broadening CPI gives CAD a floor and trims dovish-BoC conviction — but oil ~$74 (3-mo low) re-presses the export side. Net less heavy than Sunday. Next BoC late Jul.

strength.drivers: Core ~2.3%; unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 11, dovish 'no cycle' frame).
strength.forward: ⚖️↘ Pure USD-leg: hawkish Fed pushes EUR/USD down, dovish ECB frame caps bounces. Flash PMIs Tue the only local catalyst. Next ECB late Jul.

symbols.why: Cleanest short-USD leg: hawkish-repriced dollar (DXY 1-yr high) vs an ECB that's done — but crowded into PCE.
symbols.risk: A soft core PCE (Thu) or a deal-implementation risk-on wave bounces it; trail stops.

symbols.why: Two-sided: oil ~$74 (3-mo low) re-presses CAD, but the broadening CPI floor (ex-gas 2.2%) caps the upside push — long but not one-way.
symbols.risk: Oil re-spike on a 60-day-roadmap breakdown; a gold bid; soft US PCE caps it.

symbols.why: Trimmed: cleanest commodity split still favours AUD, but CAD no longer a pushover after the broadening CPI; risk-on aids AUD beta.
symbols.risk: Soft Aussie CPI/jobs; a CAD-supportive oil bid; risk-off on a deal collapse hits AUD beta harder.

symbols.risk: Firmer CAD / oil-gold bid fights it; an MoF spike snaps it lower abruptly (the re-load).

symbols.why: ECB spent vs an NZD floored by firm Q1 GDP — slight NZD tilt.

symbols.why: Both commodity-bloc, both floored (RBA hawkish-hold vs RBNZ firm GDP) — a wash.

symbols.why: Now the STRONG leg: 1-yr high ~100.85 on the hawkish repricing that stuck into the weekend.

symbols.why: Base case institutionalised (formal 60-day roadmap, Hormuz toll-free 60 days, oil ~$74) — the tail keeps shrinking; hawkish-Fed rates drag the only cap left.

catalysts.note: First post-cluster growth pulse; the EUR/GBP local catalyst.
Tests the RBA's 'inflation risks up' lean — the AUD swing.
Direct test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call; a soft core is the dollar's only near-term fade. Median '26 PCE 3.6%, core 3.3%.

catalysts.event: Australia jobs · Japan CPI · final US GDP
catalysts.note: AUD labour swing; JPY pipeline check.
USD/JPY ~161.5, FinMin Katayama verbal-only. Biggest near-term swing for the whole JPY book.
Formalised: working groups + Hormuz toll-free 60 days; technical talks continue at the Bürgenstock. A breakdown is the residual oil-respike tail.
NZD's next live event; hike case fading on oil.
All next decisions parked to late July.

geopolitics: Base case institutionalised Monday. The US and Iran agreed a 60-day roadmap to a final deal (oversight/sanctions/nuclear working groups), agreed Hormuz stays toll-free for at least 60 days, and the US Treasury authorised Iranian oil production/sale for 60 days; Araghchi called Switzerland talks 'major progress'. The IRGC's Saturday re-closure claim was flatly denied by the US military ('Iran does not control the Strait'); markets sided with the flows — Iran is shipping the most oil through Hormuz since the conflict began, Kuwait/ADNOC restoring output. WTI sank to ~$74.3 (lowest since early March; Brent Aug ~$80.3); equities stayed risk-on; gold held ~$4,195. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Tail smaller but not dead: a 60-day-roadmap breakdown / Lebanon re-escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,195 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
